$comment: Machine-readable surface for a future /apply-pattern installer. Human spec: patterns/provider-neutral-adapter/pattern.md. Expressed AS an invariant cluster (see docs/patterns-registry.md#patterns-as-clusters-of-invariants). Promoted from candidate to specified; realized twice in dogeared-coach (video + billing).
title: Provider-Neutral Port/Adapter
applies_when: a dependency on an external service you might swap (billing, video/media, email, sms, storage, search)
you want the vendor's credentials + failure modes contained behind one boundary
signed webhooks, per-request tokens, or direct browser->CDN upload are involved
usually stamped early: it's a dependency of the money/media-path patterns
components: single-file-vendor-confinement: the vendor SDK is imported in exactly one file (+ tests); import-lint enforceable; no vendor type crosses the seam

neutral-dtos-unexported-concrete: port interface exposes only vendor-agnostic types; concrete adapter unexported with a compile-time var _ Port = (*concrete)(nil) assertion; a Fake behind the same port for testability
vendor-state-to-enum-safe-default: external status strings mapped to the app's enum at the seam; unknown state -> safe default, never leaks up raw

signed-webhook-is-truth: inbound webhooks verified (raw-body signature + replay window) and normalized to a neutral internal event {id,type,livemode,raw}; downstream reads that, never the client redirect
sign-per-request-token: per-request access token minted at the seam, never persisted, TTL clamped to a hard ceiling
direct-browser-to-cdn-upload: large uploads go browser->vendor via a one-time server-minted ticket; the API credential never reaches the browser

invariants.$comment: The cluster this pattern guarantees. Generic ids are the pattern's own; realized_as cites the provenance app's code. Realized twice (video via Cloudflare, billing via Stripe).

cluster.statement: Single-file vendor confinement: vendor SDK imported in exactly one file (+ tests); no vendor type crosses the seam.
Neutral DTOs only; concrete adapter unexported + compile-time var _ Port = (*concrete)(nil); a Fake behind the same port.
Vendor status mapped to an internal enum with a safe default for unknown states.

realized_as.code: billing status->tier + video status mapping at the seam

cluster.statement: Signed webhook normalized to a neutral internal event {id,type,livemode,raw}; the webhook, not the client redirect, is source of truth.
Sign-per-request access token: minted per request, never persisted, TTL clamped to a hard ceiling.
Direct browser->CDN upload via a one-time server-minted ticket; the API credential never reaches the browser.

vendor.description: External service behind the seam (billing / video / email ...).

port.description: The neutral interface name callers depend on.

confinement_file.description: The single file allowed to import the vendor SDK; import-lint target.

webhook_events.description: Vendor events normalized to neutral internal events.

token_ttl_ceiling.description: Hard cap on sign-per-request token TTL.

direct_upload.description: Whether large uploads go browser->CDN via a one-time ticket.

metrics.desc: vendor types outside the confinement file (target 0; import-lint asserted)
files that must change to swap the vendor (target 1)
flows where a long-lived vendor credential reaches the client (target 0)

protected_paths: the confinement file (the one vendor-SDK importer)

feeds.fetch-on-webhook-reconcile: its 'webhook is source of truth' ingestion is INV-PNA-004 applied to the billing vendor
feeds.tiered-subscription: billing vendor + signed webhooks sit behind this seam
feeds.elevated-access-session: the session signer / identity provider sit behind this seam
feeds.multi-tenant-isolation: media/CDN per-request tokens are minted after the tenant/authorization guards
